title: LearnVisualiseFlow
description: A traced code flow, broken into ordered steps. Each step is grounded in a real file + function from the codebase. The renderer (template.html) consumes this directly.

flow_title.description: Short human title for the whole flow, e.g. 'Google sign-in flow' or 'Video homepage feed'.

summary.description: 1-2 sentence plain-language overview of what this flow does end to end.

systems.description: The distinct systems/layers this flow touches, in the order they first appear. Used to build the legend. Each must reference a key from the fixed system enum below.

steps.description: Ordered steps of the flow. Each step is one meaningful unit of work in one system.

system.description: Which system this step happens in. Drives the color + icon in the renderer.

title.description: Short imperative/declarative title, <=8 words. e.g. 'Middleware verifies the JWT'.

detail.description: 2-4 sentences explaining what happens here and why, in plain language. This is the 'explanation box' that travels with the data.

file.description: Real repo-relative path of the file where this happens, e.g. 'backend/src/middleware/verifyToken.ts'. Use '(external)' for third-party systems with no local file.

fn.description: The actual function / route / method name involved, e.g. 'verifyFirebaseToken(req, reply)' or 'POST /auth/session'.

data.description: Optional. What data is flowing OUT of this step into the next, e.g. 'Firebase ID token (JWT)' or 'verified UID'. Rendered on the connector between steps.

branch.description: Optional. A failure / alternate path off this step (e.g. token invalid -> 401). Rendered as a side-branch.
